Correct answer: D
Solution

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

An ideal gas (R = 0.287 kJ/kgK) is flowing through an insulated duct at the rate of 4 kg/s. The temperature of the environment is 27°C. If the pressure drop from inlet to the exit of the pipe is 20%, the rate of exergy loss due to friction at steady state is ______ kW. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 76.85
Solution

76.85 (75.00 - 78.00)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A heat pump is used to heat a room. The output surrounding temperature is –40°C and the inside room temperature is 25°C. Assuming both the temperatures remains constant. If a power of 100 kW supplied to the heat pump, the maximum heat transferred to the room is _____ kW.
(Round off to 2 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 458.46
Solution

458.46 (450.00 - 465.00)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The gear box of a machine is operating under steady state receives 50 kW of power from input shaft and transmits 46 kW to the output shaft. If the surface temperature of gear box is at 70°C and loses heat to surrounding at 30°C, the exergy loss in the gear box is ____ kW. (Round off to 3 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 3.533
Solution

3.533 (3.500 - 3.570)

Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are CORRECT?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)
• The loss of available energy is more, when heat loss occurs at a higher temperature than when the same heat loss occurs at a lower temperature.
• While energy is always conserved, exergy is not generally conserved, but is destroyed by irreversibilities.
• The exergy of an isolated system can never increase.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Five kg of water at 30°C are adiabatically mixed with two kg of water at 90°C in a chamber with constant pressure of 1 atm. Assume the specific heat of water at constant pressure, cp = 4.18 kJ/kgK. The increase in entropy of total mass of water due to the mixing process is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Correct answer: A

(a)

Consider a rigid insulated tank containing air (ratio of specific heats = 1.4) at 350 K and 1 bar. The tank is connected to a pipeline with pressurized air at 350 K and 10 bar. If pressurized air is allowed to enter and stopped when the mass of air inside the tank doubles, its temperature is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Correct answer: C
Solution

Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Two kg of air (gas constant, R = 0.287 kJ/kgK; specific heats, = 0.718 kJ/kgK, cp = 1.005 kJ/kgK) is at 900 kPa and 700 K. If ambient condition is 100 kPa and 300 K, the maximum amount of work that can be obtained from the air is ___ kJ. (Round off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 314.3
Solution

314.3 (312 - 316)

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

(Round off 1 decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 6.4
Solution

6.4 (5.4 - 7.4)

Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Steam enters a nozzle at 300°C and 500 kPa and leaves at 20 kPa. The following information is available on inlet and exit of nozzle from the steam table.

If the nozzle increases the kinetic energy of steam by 520 kJ/kg and ambient temperature is 30°C, the second law of efficiency of the nozzle is ______ %.

(Correct upto 1 decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 86.8
Solution

86.8 (85.0 - 88.0)

Q17 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
• The entropy of an isolated system always increases and becomes a maximum at the state of equilibrium.
• Entropy is a point or state function but entropy generation is a path function.
• There is no entropy transfer associated with work transfer, even if work is dissipated adiabatically into internal energy increase of the system, there is an entropy increase in the system, but there is as such no entropy transfer to it.
